mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals rapid labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives immediate positive equity returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances across a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and rising consumer credit balances indicate synchronized expansion in durable goods demand and household leverage, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and lifts S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any rolling 12-month period, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index during a post-shock recovery environment reflects restored manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which expands profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average during this specific regime, or the predictive R-squared drops below 0.02 over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth rate signals broad economic acceleration that improves corporate earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if the sign of the 5-day forward return consistently contradicts the direction of GDP growth revisions in more than 60% of quarterly observations over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.50 percentage points over three months triggers a tightening monetary regime that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields positive or neutral 60-day forward returns in over 50% of triggered episodes, or the cross-sectional predictive power (Rank IC) remains below 0.02 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion that sustains wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate statistically significant positive alpha (t-stat < 1.96) over any 3-year rolling window, or the marginal effect of PAYEMS becomes negative when UNRATE is below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances during a late-cycle expansion phase indicate excessive household leverage that precedes a consumption pullback, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings risks materialize.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if 5-day forward returns remain positive when RSAFS exceeds its 24-month moving average during late-cycle conditions, or if the signal's hit rate does not exceed 55% in out-of-sample testing.
